explanation: This video is educational/instructional in nature, featuring section breaks, title cards, and demonstration segments rather than a continuous, timed trainee performance of a single FLS task. The performance segments visible (endoloop-like task and suturing task) are embedded within instructional framing and lack clear timing markers, task state indicators (start/stop signals), or official FLS setup. Per protocol, instructional videos are not scored for performance; teaching content is extracted instead.

demonstrated_techniques: Proper grip position on needle drivers
Needle passage through training materials (cone, Penrose drain)
VR simulation-based FLS training interface usage
teaching_content.training_focus: Fundamental laparoscopic manipulation skills using VR simulation platform, structured around hand coordination and acquire/manipulation phases

confidence_rationale: Classification confidence is moderate because the video contains recognizable FLS training elements (Penrose drain, needle drivers, VR interface) but lacks the formal structure and continuous timing of an official FLS assessment. The mixing of title cards and instructional overlays with embedded performance segments indicates educational content rather than proctored performance.
